title: dalea glumacea
intro: Dalea glumacea, also known as the white prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antispasmodic and expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, that can help soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Dalea glumacea is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications.
